subject: Sentinel advisor — tool contract monitoring, KPI health tracking, and self-healing escalation
title: Sentinel Advisor — Tool Contracts, KPI Monitoring, Dynamic Frequency, and Escalation Path
what: Defines the Sentinel advisor: the tool health and system integrity monitor for the Wheel. Sentinel maintains contracts for every tool in the spoke's ToolGraph — each contract declares expected latency, success_rate, and output_shape. Sentinel measures KPIs on every tool call, adjusts monitoring frequency dynamically (increases after recent events, reduces when stable), and dispatches remediation via a typed escalation path: software defects create bug lugs dispatched to AP; configuration issues create task lugs; issues outside agent authority are escalated to Mario as Wilbur proposals. Sentinel + the evolution mechanism form a closed self-improvement loop: the Wheel monitors itself, identifies degradation, and routes fixes without human triage.
why: Without Sentinel, tool failures are discovered reactively — a session fails and the user notices. With Sentinel, degradation is detected proactively via KPI drift before sessions fail. The escalation path (software → impl lug, config → task lug, hardware/external → Mario proposal) maps every failure mode to an appropriate response. The dynamic monitoring frequency prevents Sentinel from burning tokens on stable tools while increasing vigilance when anomalies appear. Sentinel + Assessor + Navigator together give the Wheel a complete observability stack: Assessor tracks model quality, Navigator tracks resource/cost, Sentinel tracks tool health.
audience: Sentinel advisor process — runs at session startup and on tool call completion
AP — receives Sentinel alerts as impl/task lugs; decides whether to immediately act or queue
Mario — receives escalations for issues outside agent authority (external provider outages, hardware bounds)
Navigator — reads Sentinel ToolGraph health annotations to avoid routing work to degraded tools
Herald daemon — Sentinel monitors Herald's poll health (last_poll_at, consecutive_failures)
use_cases: Proactive degradation detection: anthropic-sonnet latency climbs from 2s to 8s over 3 sessions; Sentinel detects drift before a session timeout; creates task lug 'Investigate Anthropic latency spike'
Success rate breach: Supabase REST success_rate drops to 60% (contract says >=95%); Sentinel creates bug lug dispatched to AP; AP diagnoses and fixes connection pooling
Output shape drift: a tool that previously returned {status, data} now returns {status, payload}; Sentinel detects schema mismatch; creates impl lug to update callers

External provider outage: Anthropic API returns 503 for 15 minutes; issue is outside agent authority; Sentinel creates Wilbur proposal 'Anthropic outage — consider switching to local Ollama temporarily'
Dynamic frequency: supabase-rest had 3 failures in last hour; Sentinel increases monitoring from every-5th-call to every-call; reverts after 30 stable calls
Herald health: Herald has not polled for 5 minutes (2x its 30s interval); Sentinel alerts; creates task lug 'Herald daemon appears stalled'
patterns: Contract-per-tool: every entry in ToolGraph.json access_matrix has a matching Sentinel contract in sentinel-contracts.json
KPI measurement on completion: after every tool call, Sentinel records latency, success/failure, and output schema fingerprint
Dynamic frequency: base_frequency (default: every 5th call); increases to every call when consecutive_failures > 0 or recent anomaly; reverts after stability_window stable calls
Escalation decision tree: is the fix code? → bug lug. Is it config? → task lug. Is it outside agent authority (provider outage, hardware, billing)? → Wilbur proposal
Sentinel is non-blocking: measurement and alerting are asynchronous; Sentinel never blocks a tool call; it observes after the call completes
Stability window: Sentinel reduces frequency only after stability_window consecutive calls succeed with KPIs within contract bounds

kpis.max_latency_ms: int — p95 latency ceiling; breach triggers alert
kpis.min_success_rate: float 0-1 — fraction of calls that must succeed over window_calls
kpis.output_schema_fingerprint: string | null — SHA of expected output JSON schema; null if output is variable

tool_contract.measurement_window_calls: int default 20 — rolling window for success_rate calculation
tool_contract.base_monitoring_frequency: int default 5 — measure every Nth call
tool_contract.stability_window_calls: int default 30 — consecutive successes needed to reduce frequency

software_defect.condition: output schema mismatch, logic error in caller code, unexpected error type from agent code

configuration_issue.condition: wrong endpoint, missing env var, misconfigured connection pool, wrong model_id

external_provider_issue.condition: provider 5xx, rate limit from provider, billing suspension, hardware-bound latency

constraints: Sentinel is non-blocking: it MUST NOT add latency to tool calls; measurement happens post-call in async context
Sentinel MUST have a contract for every tool in ToolGraph.json access_matrix; tools without contracts are flagged as unknown health
Auto-created bug/task lugs from Sentinel must include: tool_id, contract_kpi_violated, observed_value, window_data snapshot, and a suggested perceive section
Sentinel must not create duplicate lugs for the same tool and same breach within a single session — deduplicate by (tool_id, kpi_type, session_id)
Dynamic frequency adjustments persist across session restarts via sentinel-contracts.json current_monitoring_frequency
Wilbur proposals from Sentinel require at least 3 consecutive breach measurements before auto-creation — prevents false alarms from transient spikes
tests: Contract breach detection: inject tool call with latency 3x max_latency_ms; verify Sentinel records breach and creates a lug
Success rate breach: inject 5 failures in a 20-call window (25%); min_success_rate=0.95; verify breach detected, lug created
Output schema drift: tool starts returning {payload:...} instead of {data:...}; verify fingerprint mismatch detected
Dynamic frequency increase: 2 consecutive failures; verify current_monitoring_frequency reset to 1 (every call)
Dynamic frequency decrease: 30 consecutive successes after frequency increase; verify frequency reverts to base_monitoring_frequency
Escalation routing: external provider 503 error; verify lug type=task with routed_to=WILBUR created, not a bug lug
Deduplication: same tool breaches twice in one session; verify only one lug created
Wilbur proposal threshold: only 2 consecutive breaches; verify no Wilbur proposal yet; third breach triggers creation

open_questions: How does Sentinel intercept tool calls in Claude Code? No hook exists for post-tool-call measurement today. Does this require a new hook type (PostToolUse), or does Sentinel scan session transcripts retroactively?
output_schema_fingerprint: SHA of JSON schema requires Sentinel to know the schema upfront. For tools with variable output, what is the practical measurement?
Wilbur proposals: 'WILBUR' is not a current routed_to value. Does this need a new routing target, or does it route to Mario via a different mechanism (notification, lug delivered to user inbox)?

perceive: Read this spec. Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/sentinel/ directory if it exists. Read WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json for the access_matrix of tools that need contracts. Read spec-toolgraph-v1 for tool_id schema. Check .claude/hooks/ for any existing PostToolUse hook that could carry KPI measurement.
execute: 1. Create WAI-Spoke/advisors/sentinel/ directory. 2. Create sentinel-contracts.json with initial contracts for all tools in ToolGraph.json access_matrix. 3. Implement kpi_record() function: called post-tool-use, appends to kpi-log.jsonl, checks against contract, adjusts frequency, creates escalation lugs on breach. 4. Wire kpi_record() to PostToolUse hook (or retroactive scan at session end if hook not available). 5. Implement Wilbur proposal creation (define WILBUR routing or use task lug with tag=wilbur-proposal). 6. Add Sentinel startup check: load contracts, report current_health for all tools in ToolGraph.
verify: 1. sentinel-contracts.json exists with at least one contract per ToolGraph access_matrix entry. 2. Inject a latency breach; verify kpi-log.jsonl records it. 3. Verify bug lug auto-created on code-level breach. 4. Verify Wilbur proposal on external provider breach. 5. Frequency correctly increases after failure, decreases after stability window.
